127285.explanation: A mix of men and women is our preference. But ultimately, the quality to govern must always come first.

127286.explanation: It is important that residents participate in the conversation and receive good information. We consider each time what the most suitable form is.

127289.explanation: We encourage small-scale initiatives from farms. However, for each initiative, we assess whether it fits within the environment.

127290.explanation: This is not a task of the municipality. However, we do cooperate with such initiatives and facilitate obtaining subsidies.

127291.explanation: No XXL distribution block boxes added. But there is room for other new companies where more Wijchen residents can work.

127292.explanation: Wijchen must comply with the legal obligation for the reception of refugees (the distribution law).

127294.explanation: Sometimes trees have to be felled for, for example, extra housing. We want to continue investing in a vital and green living environment; therefore, we want to plant at least two trees for every tree cut down.

127297.explanation: If the national ban comes into effect, we want to enable initiatives for such a show. Initially, it is not the municipality's role to organize this.

127298.explanation: Bulky waste can be dropped off at the recycling center. To counteract litter nuisance, we want to explain the system more clearly and help people who cannot bring it themselves. We want to strictly enforce rules against those who refuse.

127299.explanation: Events contribute to social encounters and we want to facilitate them. We want to limit nuisance as much as possible. Noise standards are generally well established but, of course, must be enforced.

127300.explanation: The municipality is responsible for the accessibility of municipal buildings. We also find the accessibility of commercial buildings important, but that is the responsibility of the entrepreneur.

127301.explanation: This is already possible. However, we want to be cautious about building in the rural area. The pressure on this space is increasing. We believe the rural area should primarily retain its functions for agriculture, recreation, energy, water, and nature.

127302.explanation: For residents with a low income, there is the refund scheme. This contribution can be used to cover part of the membership fees. For children, there is the child scheme, a contribution for the costs of school, sports, or hobbies.

127307.explanation: We want to protect monuments, unique village views, and other valuable elements. They give the municipality its own identity and make people feel at home. We do not want to cut back on this.

127309.explanation: We use camera surveillance only where necessary and only as long as needed.

127312.explanation: Yes, we want to give priority when the opportunity arises (such as with the 28 apartments on Meidoornstraat, Valendries district). At the same time, there is no fence around Wijchen.

127313.explanation: There is an increasing demand for smaller homes for both the elderly and younger people. The number of one- and two-person households keeps growing.

127314.explanation: There is also demand for holiday homes. Holiday homes are often located in rural areas, farther from facilities, and mainly contribute to recreation and tourism in our region. Therefore, we do not support their use as a permanent residence.

127315.explanation: We are cautious about building in the rural area. For new homes, we focus on space in and directly adjacent to the villages, fitting the size and scale of the village. We do not exclude building directly outside the existing village core.

127316.explanation: We also understand that this is not possible for everyone and want to invest mainly in education and guidance so that everyone can participate.

127317.explanation: Waiving fees must remain possible but only in special cases. So not waiving fees as a rule, but customization remains possible.

127320.explanation: Yes, more charging points must be installed because the number of electric cars is still growing. When more charging stations become available, exclusive use can, in our opinion, end, and the parking space may also be used by non-electric cars.

127321.explanation: In residential neighborhoods, the maximum speed is already set at 30 km/h. The speed on the larger arterial roads, in our view, does not need to be lowered and remains at a maximum of 50 km/h.

127322.explanation: Care is costly and scarce. Therefore, it is not possible to accommodate every wish. The idea is sympathetic but not feasible.

127325.explanation: Our municipality has excellent facilities for dog owners, such as designated dog-walking areas and off-leash zones. These must be maintained and therefore cost money. We think it is logical that dog owners pay for this.

127326.explanation: Tourist tax is paid by guests of hotels and campsites. This allows the municipality to pay for extra facilities for tourists. We do not rule out a slight increase.

127329.explanation: Wijchen must comply with the legal obligation for housing status holders and assigns housing within the legal frameworks.

127331.explanation: At this moment, we have sufficient solar fields (some in preparation). Although we don’t know what the future holds, we are cautious about using even more valuable agricultural land. Space in the rural area is scarce.

127335.explanation: Kernachtig Wijchen considers it important that Wijchen has its own indoor swimming pool. We believe this new pool should meet the wishes of our residents, the active swimming clubs, and visitors. The new pool should be in or close to the core of Wijchen.

127285.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. With as many men as women. Such a government represents all perspectives to make fair decisions. Therefore, we strive for an equal distribution.

127286.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When a topic is of great concern, a referendum can be a nice and fair way to make a decision. This way, we involve people in local democracy. This can be done, for example, via a citizens' assembly.

127289.explanation: Agriculture must become more sustainable, but we also want farmers to be able to earn money with their farms. A shop with good products from their own land or a nice campsite can help with that. Therefore, we want to make these options possible-with care for nature (for example, a mini-campsite).

127290.explanation: This is the ideal picture: residents jointly produce their own green and affordable electricity with a wind turbine or solar panels. They use the electricity onsite, so the grid is not burdened. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality and should be encouraged.

127291.explanation: In recent years, far too many huge distribution centers have been built in the Netherlands. They spoil the landscape and much of the work is done by poorly paid labor migrants. We want to stop more distribution centers from arriving. This also protects our small retailers.

127292.explanation: Wijchen has taken the path for a reception center for asylum seekers, and we think this is a good choice. This fulfills our legal obligation under the Distribution Law.

127294.explanation: The Netherlands is a crowded country, and we must protect the green space we have. Greenery is essential for cooling, clean air, and a pleasant neighborhood. Animals and other plants cannot live without trees. That is why we say: for every tree felled, two new plants.

127297.explanation: The best New Year's celebration is one we all celebrate together, with one big fireworks show or, preferably, a laser or drone show organized by the municipality. This keeps it safe and allows us to enjoy a cozy New Year's Eve together.

127298.explanation: Garbage, old mattresses, and other litter on the street are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. The easiest way to prevent this is by collecting bulky waste for free. This avoids hassle and gives us cleaner streets.

127299.explanation: Noise standards do not need to become stricter, but the current standards must be better enforced.

127300.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. However, many important places are not yet properly accessible, for example for wheelchair users. Entrepreneurs often cannot afford such adaptations on their own. Therefore, we believe the municipality should provide support.

127301.explanation: There is an enormous housing shortage, and an extra home on the farmyard can be a good solution. Either as an additional source of income or as a suitable place for a parent who can no longer live independently. We gladly make this possible for farmers who want it.

127302.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy and satisfaction. And it keeps you healthy. But for people with welfare benefits, sports are often too expensive. We believe the municipality should ensure this group can participate in a sports club for free.

127307.explanation: Our monuments make our municipality beautiful and unique. We believe they must be well cared for. It would be a tremendous loss if unique places are lost forever because we are currently choosing to make budget cuts.

127309.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But we do not believe more cameras are the right solution. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be infringed upon lightly. Therefore, we prefer more enforcement officers (if necessary) who can immediately intervene if something goes wrong.

127312.explanation: This does not solve the housing problem and limits the possibilities of all home-seekers who want to move to another municipality.

127313.explanation: The housing market is completely gridlocked, largely because older people cannot move to smaller homes. That's why we want to build that type of housing as soon as possible. This will also free up houses for other home-seekers.

127314.explanation: Holiday homes are not a permanent solution for the housing shortage. But as long as the need is high, some people can use them. It is important that they can use the holiday home as a fixed address for as long as necessary.

127315.explanation: We want to preserve green spaces and oppose sacrificing nature for housing development. However, farmland is not nature, which is why we support using farmland for housing. Although we can still develop within certain areas, it is important to create green neighborhoods.

127316.explanation: People are not on social assistance because they do not want to work. They need a stable foundation (such as a basic job), affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

127317.explanation: If debt forgiveness of municipal taxes stops, people with the lowest incomes in our municipality will get into big trouble. They simply cannot miss that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water, which is why we support debt forgiveness.

127320.explanation: Electric transport is good for the environment and the economy. Electric charging must be easily accessible, which encourages the use of electric cars.

127321.explanation: The figures clearly show: a lower maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with reduced mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

127322.explanation: Good care comes first, regardless of faith, culture, or belief. We encourage customized care, but it is not the starting point.

127325.explanation: The dog tax causes a lot of bureaucracy, and pets, including dogs, are important companions for many people. We share the costs for dog walking areas and similar facilities together.

127326.explanation: In Wijchen, the tourist tax is relatively low, and nature is under pressure. We use the additional income from the tourist tax for nature conservation.

127329.explanation: Refugees have a vulnerable position that we must help if possible. We give refugees who fall within the municipality’s quota priority for decent housing, just like other urgent home-seekers.

127331.explanation: A field full of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want, they should be able to use their land for this. Moreover, solar panels can be well combined with more biodiversity, for example by growing flowers and other low greenery.

127335.explanation: A shared swimming pool saves a lot of costs and offers more sports and recreational opportunities.

127285.explanation: We stumble over the word 'must'. Of course, we want a diverse and inclusive council that goes beyond just gender. Think of background, disabilities, etc. An equal number of men and women is of course very desirable.

127286.explanation: Council members are democratically elected and make careful considerations based on the full context. Important issues cannot be reduced to a simple yes or no.

127289.explanation: The CDA supports diversification of the farming business, such as farm shops or small-scale recreation. This strengthens the countryside. But it must fit the environment and traffic safety. Allow space where possible, set limits where necessary.

127290.explanation: The CDA supports local energy cooperatives because they involve residents in sustainability. But we do not provide financial support: the municipality must use resources purposefully and not saddle residents with extra costs. We do support through advice and regulation.

127291.explanation: The CDA does not want new large distribution centers. They put heavy pressure on space, traffic, and landscape. We choose to strengthen the local economy and businesses that provide jobs and livability. Space is scarce and must be used carefully.

127292.explanation: The municipality must implement the Spread Law. If shelter is needed, we take responsibility. A refugee center can be accommodated if appropriate in scale, well integrated, and in consultation with residents. This way we contribute fairly to humane and careful reception.

127294.explanation: The CDA supports this statement: trees are important for a green, healthy living environment. Replanting every cut tree keeps Wijchen green, strengthens biodiversity, and helps against heat stress. This keeps neighborhoods livable and future-proof.

127297.explanation: The CDA supports an alternative to private fireworks. This facilitates a smooth transition to a total fireworks ban. The municipality also focuses on enforcement, safety, and clear rules.

127298.explanation: The CDA does not want bulky waste to be collected for free at home. This leads to high costs that everyone must pay, even those who do not use the service. We choose good, affordable drop-off facilities and encourage reuse so that waste costs for residents remain manageable.

127299.explanation: The CDA finds the risk of hearing damage under current noise standards too high. There is also a problem of nuisance for the environment. We want the municipality to supervise the agreements more clearly.

127300.explanation: The CDA finds accessibility important but does not see it as the municipality’s task to financially support entrepreneurs directly. The municipality assists with information, advice, and stimulating measures. Entrepreneurs remain responsible for the accessibility of their premises.

127301.explanation: The CDA wants to give farmers more space to develop further. Under the KPI scheme, two homes may be built on their own farmyard. This keeps the business future-proof and the countryside livable - within clear spatial frameworks and with attention to the environment.

127302.explanation: The CDA wants everyone to be able to play sports. Through tailored measures within the minimum policy, the municipality can reimburse a sports membership.

127307.explanation: CDA Wijchen wants to preserve monuments. Budget cuts on monument care undermine our history and identity. We choose preservation, support for owners, and careful sustainability.

127309.explanation: CDA Wijchen sees camera surveillance as a good tool in places with lots of nuisance. It helps increase safety, deter offenders, and resolve problems faster. Where needed, we deploy cameras selectively to keep neighborhoods and the center livable.

127312.explanation: CDA Wijchen finds it important that the residents of Wijchen can find a home in their own environment.

127313.explanation: CDA Wijchen believes more small rental and owner-occupied homes for seniors should be built. This allows seniors to move to suitable homes, frees up space for families, and keeps Wijchen livable and balanced.

127314.explanation: CDA Wijchen does not want holiday homes to be automatically allowed as a permanent address. It reduces the recreational function, puts pressure on nature and facilities, and does not structurally solve the housing challenge. We opt for real housing and clear frameworks for recreation.

127315.explanation: CDA Wijchen wants building within village boundaries where possible. For livability and housing flow, expansion on the edges is sometimes necessary, fitting the scale and character of our villages.

127316.explanation: CDA Wijchen believes people on social assistance should not be forced into 'work in return' that does not contribute to their prospects. We believe in tailor-made approaches: meaningful activities can help but must always be appropriate, voluntary, and aimed at participation and progress.

127317.explanation: CDA Wijchen is in favor of debt relief for residents with low incomes. This scheme prevents people with financial problems from falling deeper into debt. We remain committed to basic security and support where it is truly needed.

127320.explanation: CDA Wijchen wants more charging points. We expand chargers purposefully where demand is high and combine this with smart charging. This keeps neighborhoods accessible and encourages sustainable mobility. Moreover, a charging point is also a parking spot.

127321.explanation: CDA Wijchen finds 30 km/h not desirable everywhere. Traffic safety is paramount, but a uniform speed reduction does not fit all roads. We choose tailor-made solutions: 30 km/h where it is truly safer, and 50 km/h where roads are designed for it. This keeps traffic safe and flowing.

127322.explanation: CDA Wijchen believes people who need care should be able to choose as much as possible a care provider who fits their faith, culture, or belief. Care should match who you are. At the same time, quality, availability, and affordability remain leading.

127325.explanation: CDA Wijchen does not want to abolish the dog tax. The revenues are used purposefully and are financially necessary. For owners, the dog tax is also only a relatively small part of the total costs of keeping a dog.

127326.explanation: CDA Wijchen does not want to increase the tourist tax just like that. Recreation is important for our local economy and must remain affordable. We choose a fair balance: only increase if it is demonstrably necessary and does not harm the sector.

127329.explanation: In the Netherlands, status holders legally have priority for social rental housing to speed up outflow from shelters. Municipalities, including Wijchen, implement this within national frameworks while simultaneously seeking balance with other housing seekers.

127331.explanation: Solar panels should be placed as much as possible on roofs. Agricultural land remains primarily for food production and landscape. Only in extreme cases can installation on land be allowed, provided it is carefully considered and with strict conditions for nature, livability, and spatial quality.

127335.explanation: The idea of one new swimming pool with Beuningen is no longer on the table. A principle decision would be supported by CDA Wijchen to explore cooperation, leaving room to choose differently later based on costs, facilities, and support.

127285.explanation: The VVD Wijchen believes that quality and suitability for the position are more important than a mandatory male-female ratio. In our municipality, this means that when appointing the mayor and aldermen, suitability is considered, not gender.

127286.explanation: The VVD Wijchen supports citizen participation. This occurs through the elected municipal council so that decisions are made democratically and carefully.

127289.explanation: The VVD Wijchen believes farmers should have room to undertake business. In our municipality, this means initiatives such as a shop or campsite are possible within reasonable rules.

127290.explanation: The VVD Wijchen believes sustainable energy is important. Its generation, organization, and financing-whether individually or collectively-are up to residents or entrepreneurs.

127291.explanation: The VVD Wijchen opts for economic growth and a strong local labor market. Therefore, the establishment of new distribution centers is not prevented upfront but aligned with the municipal plan for business parks.

127292.explanation: The VVD Wijchen chooses responsibility in shelter. Therefore, an asylum seekers’ center (AZC) can be established in Wijchen, provided it is not large-scale and well supervised, so both residents and asylum seekers benefit from a safe and livable environment. It can happen, but it is not mandatory.

127294.explanation: The VVD Wijchen values a green living environment. In our municipality, this means that when trees are cut down, the overall picture and quality of the living environment are considered, and new trees are planted where possible, but not mandatory for every felled tree.

127297.explanation: The VVD Wijchen values traditions. The municipality facilitates organizing fireworks shows. As long as this fits within laws and regulations and enforcement is guaranteed, neighborhoods can organize these themselves.

127298.explanation: The VVD Wijchen adheres to the principle 'the polluter pays.' Therefore, bulky waste is not collected for free at home but can be brought to the recycling center. Residents pay a contribution to cover costs and finance waste processing.

127299.explanation: The VVD Wijchen believes noise standards must be clear. These must be well enforced and monitored so that both residents and entrepreneurs see their interests protected.

127300.explanation: The VVD Wijchen supports the importance of accessibility. The municipality ensures that public buildings are accessible so public resources are used responsibly.

127301.explanation: The VVD Wijchen supports all developments for small-scale housing construction. This enables more homes to become available quickly in our municipality.

127302.explanation: The VVD Wijchen believes sports are important for everyone but remain a personal responsibility. In Wijchen, a budget is available that can be used more broadly than just sports and fits the needs of the family.

127307.explanation: The VVD Wijchen chooses a financially healthy municipality. Therefore, all expenditures must be critically reviewed, including monument care.

127309.explanation: The VVD Wijchen opts for a safe municipality. Therefore, more camera surveillance must be installed so that nuisance and crime can be effectively addressed, and the deterrent effect of cameras contributes to residents’ safety.

127312.explanation: The VVD Wijchen stands for the interests of local residents and acknowledges there are always urgent priority groups. Therefore, housing seekers from Wijchen receive partial priority for social rental homes, so local residents find housing faster without urgent cases missing out.

127313.explanation: The VVD Wijchen chooses to build according to demand and need. Therefore, the type of houses to be built must match the actual local demand so that people quickly have suitable housing to start or move on.

127314.explanation: The VVD Wijchen opts for a balance between a healthy housing market and flexibility in exceptional situations. Therefore, a holiday home may temporarily be used as a residence in cases of urgency and need, such as a divorce.

127315.explanation: The VVD Wijchen chooses to build, build, build to tackle the housing shortage. Therefore, new construction must also be possible outside existing village boundaries. We look beyond borders and do not think in limitations.

127316.explanation: The VVD Wijchen chooses responsibility and participation. Therefore, people receiving social assistance who do not have paid work must contribute to society in other ways, so everyone participates and contributes where possible.

127317.explanation: The VVD Wijchen opts for a fair and financially healthy municipality. Therefore, the municipality must stop routinely waiving taxes and only provide exceptions in cases of real financial hardship, so municipal resources are used fairly and efficiently.

127320.explanation: The VVD Wijchen opts for smart and affordable mobility solutions. Therefore, the municipality should not automatically convert more parking spaces into charging points but do so based on demand to use public space efficiently.

127321.explanation: The VVD Wijchen chooses safety and accessibility. The speed limit within built-up areas should not be lowered arbitrarily. Traffic flow is important, and dangerous situations must be addressed.

127322.explanation: The VVD Wijchen supports personal choice in care, provided it fits within existing care offerings and affordable frameworks, so everyone can receive appropriate care. Personal preferences should not lead to extra costs for the municipality.

127325.explanation: The VVD Wijchen adheres to the 'polluter pays' principle. Therefore, the dog tax must remain so owners contribute to cleaning up waste and maintaining public spaces.

127326.explanation: The VVD Wijchen opts for a targeted use of municipal funds. The tourist tax must be used for tourism to stimulate tourism and not to cover budget gaps.

127329.explanation: The VVD Wijchen supports the interests of local residents and recognizes the urgency of status holders. Therefore, status holders allocated to Wijchen receive partial priority for social rental homes, balanced with people on the waiting list.

127331.explanation: The VVD Wijchen opts for a smart and efficient approach to sustainability. Therefore, solar panels must first be placed on roofs and agricultural land only used in exceptional cases to preserve agricultural land.

127335.explanation: The VVD Wijchen supports smart cooperation and efficient use of resources. Therefore, the municipality should, where possible, collaborate with other municipalities on projects so residents benefit from good and attractive facilities and finances are used effectively.

127285.explanation: D66 wants to focus on quality and equal opportunities. We choose leaders based on skill and courage; diversity matters but is broader than male/female. Therefore, no strict 50/50 requirement but an active pursuit of a representative team.

127286.explanation: D66 wants to focus on trust and involvement. Residents must have an emergency brake on major decisions, so we support a referendum. But with strict rules: only on truly important topics and with a high turnout threshold, so not just the loudest voice decides.

127289.explanation: D66 wants to focus on equal opportunities and a strong local economy. Farmers should be able to undertake and innovate, so a farm shop or small campsite can often help. But not 'always': it must fit the environment, traffic, and nature. Therefore, flexible where possible with clear rules.

127290.explanation: D66 wants to focus on a sustainable and fair future. Residents who generate energy together deserve support, but not necessarily money. We help with knowledge and expertise: clear rules, guidance with permits and legal steps, so initiatives succeed faster.

127291.explanation: D66 wants to focus on quality and a healthy living environment. Distribution centers often generate much freight traffic and few jobs but take up a lot of space. Therefore, we slow down new large box buildings and choose sustainable, high-quality companies with more work and less nuisance.

127292.explanation: D66 wants to focus on humanity and responsibility. Wijchen must also provide shelter as required by law. But it’s not only about beds: we ensure good guidance, rapid language and participation opportunities, and clear agreements with the community so that shelter and integration go hand in hand.

127294.explanation: D66 wants to focus on a green and healthy living environment. Trees are important for shade, nature, and air quality. Therefore, cutting down trees is only allowed when necessary and replanting must always occur. If that is not possible in the same place, then nearby, with species that suit the climate and soil.

127297.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize safety and preserving traditions. Since fireworks will stop anyway, we need to celebrate New Year's Eve in a new way. Therefore, we are considering a joint show without fireworks, such as a drone or light show, so everyone can participate safely.

127298.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize a clean neighborhood and personal responsibility. We encourage people to dispose of bulky waste themselves. Free home collection for everyone leads to misuse and higher costs. However, assistance should be available for those who truly cannot manage, under clear conditions.

127299.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize livability and enjoyment for everyone. Festivals should be possible, but residents must not suffer structural nuisance. Therefore, standards do not necessarily need to be stricter, but enforcement should be tighter: clear agreements beforehand, monitoring during the event, and intervention if limits are exceeded.

127300.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize equality and accessibility. Everyone should be able to enter a store or hospitality venue. We help entrepreneurs remove barriers, especially through advice, regulations, and assistance with applications. Financial support is not always necessary or the best means.

127301.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize affordable housing and careful land use. Additional housing can help, but building on agricultural land affects landscape and nature. Therefore, this can sometimes be allowed, especially on existing parcels or through reuse of farm buildings, with strict rules and proper integration.

127302.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize participation and meaningful engagement. Social assistance is a temporary safety net, but we must not let people stagnate. Therefore, the municipality should be able to reimburse sports memberships during prolonged financial hardship so participation and health remain accessible.

127307.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize care for our heritage. Monuments tell the story of Wijchen and make the environment attractive. Cutting budgets harms maintenance and restoration, and that damage is more expensive later. Therefore, no cuts in monument care, so future generations can also enjoy them.

127309.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize both safety and privacy. Camera surveillance is a serious measure. Therefore, it should only be temporary and in exceptional cases, at locations where it demonstrably helps. Always with clear objectives, good information for residents, evaluation, and stopping as soon as it is no longer needed.

127312.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize equal opportunities and freedom to live where your life is. Priority only for Wijchen residents excludes others, for example those who work here or have family.

127313.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize aging comfortably and housing turnover. There are too few small rental and owner-occupied homes for the elderly. By building more of these, elderly people can live appropriately and larger family homes become available. Therefore: more life-course resistant, affordable homes close to facilities.

127314.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize fair housing. Permanent residence in holiday homes can help address the housing shortage. Therefore, we want to allow this with basic rules for safety and livability, as a supplement to new construction.

127315.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize affordable housing and a future-proof municipality. Building only within village boundaries is not enough to solve the housing shortage. Therefore, besides building in the core, we also carefully allow building on the edges if necessary and with good landscape integration.

127316.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize dignity and participation. We assist people from social assistance to suitable work or development. A strict obligation for “meaningful work” often backfires and does not fit everyone's situation. But we do expect commitment, with tailored support, guidance, and room for informal care or health.

127317.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize livelihood security and equal opportunities. Stopping debt remission pushes low-income people deeper into poverty. Then it becomes about survival rather than participation. Debt remission is not a luxury but a necessary support to prevent worse outcomes.

127320.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize sustainability and freedom of choice. More charging points are needed to enable electric driving. However, we keep it fair: where possible, we choose shared charging points that can also be used as regular parking spots, so parking pressure does not increase unnecessarily.

127321.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize safe and livable neighborhoods. By prioritizing pedestrians and cyclists, streets become calmer and safer, with less noise and more space to meet each other. Therefore, we support 30 km/h as the standard where the design also supports this.

127322.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize good and accessible care. Quality and safety always come first. When those are in order, it is good that people can choose a care provider that matches their faith or culture as much as possible. But “always” is not possible: availability and waiting lists also matter.

127325.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize fairness and a clean public space. If the municipality incurs costs for dog facilities and extra cleaning, it is reasonable that dog owners contribute. Therefore, do not simply abolish this but ensure revenues are visibly used for dogs.

127326.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize fairness. Tourist tax may cover costs caused by tourism, but tourists should not become cash cows. Therefore, only increase if truly necessary and well-founded, so Wijchen remains attractive and visitors spend their money with local entrepreneurs.

127329.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize responsibility and a functioning housing chain. If status holders do not move on, shelters remain occupied and costs rise. Therefore, we maintain priority so that shelters are relieved and space is created to invest in more housing and integration.

127331.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize a sustainable and future-proof municipality. Solar panels are needed, but agricultural land is scarce. Therefore, no general ban: first on roofs, parking lots, and business parks, and only on land if absolutely unavoidable, with strict rules and preserving nature and soil.

127335.explanation: D66 wants to prioritize efficient policy and good facilities. A swimming pool is important, but together with Beuningen we can share costs and improve quality. The condition is good accessibility for Wijchen.

127285.explanation: Hart voor Wijchen thinks it’s good that everyone has equal opportunities. But we do not believe there must always be an equal number of men and women in the executive board. It is more important that aldermen are competent and engaged with Wijchen.

127286.explanation: Hart voor Wijchen thinks it’s good that residents can have a say on important issues. Therefore, we want referenda to be possible, so everyone can give their opinion before decisions are made.

127289.explanation: We believe the municipality should implement as flexible a permit policy as possible, but for example a campsite must be assessed whether it fits within the environment in terms of volume and nuisance for neighbors.

127290.explanation: Hart voor Wijchen believes residents who want to jointly generate sustainable energy should receive help from the municipality. This way everyone can participate and the municipality contributes to a green future.

127291.explanation: Hart voor Wijchen believes the municipality must be careful with new distribution centers. Too many large companies can disrupt the livability and tranquility in the municipality.

127292.explanation: Hart voor Wijchen is strongly against an asylum seekers center in our municipality. We believe the influx must be sharply reduced anyway. We must be able to guarantee the safety of our residents.

127294.explanation: Hart voor Wijchen believes that for every tree cut down, a new one does not always need to be planted. It is better to consider compensation in a larger context. We want to preserve greenery as much as possible, but better from an area, nature, and recreation development perspective.

127297.explanation: As an alternative to not being allowed to set off fireworks yourself, this is a great plan. It also seems good for hospitality entrepreneurs, who benefit from the visitors who come to watch. Possibly they could contribute financially.

127298.explanation: This causes even more pollution and nuisance on the streets. The current DAR arrangement suffices in our opinion.

127299.explanation: This can sometimes be necessary, but often not. This should be assessed per area to determine what is feasible.

127300.explanation: Hart voor Wijchen believes entrepreneurs themselves are responsible for the accessibility of their building.

127301.explanation: Hart voor Wijchen believes farmers should have limited ability to build a house for their own use, but not as contractors or developers.

127302.explanation: Hart voor Wijchen believes people with social assistance benefits should have the opportunity to participate in sports. However, the municipality should not automatically pay for sports club membership; support can be arranged in a practical and targeted way.

127307.explanation: The number of monuments is very stable. Maintenance costs are rising, so cutting budgets seems quite difficult to us.

127309.explanation: Hart voor Wijchen believes it is important that residents feel safe. Therefore, the party wants more camera surveillance in places where necessary, so the municipality can better prevent and address crime.

127312.explanation: Hart voor Wijchen believes those who live or work in Wijchen should have first chance at social rental housing. This helps our own residents find a home and ensures the municipality remains a nice place to live.

127313.explanation: Hart voor Wijchen believes more small homes are needed for the elderly. Flexible homes that fit their wishes and lives are important. This way older people can continue living comfortably in Wijchen and space remains for other residents.

127314.explanation: Recreational parks are intended for recreation. If people want permanent residence, the entire park must be designated as a residential neighborhood; otherwise, we will get a form of misaligned housing.

127315.explanation: Small-scale low-rise projects should be limited as much as possible. Hart voor Wijchen believes that larger new construction projects should primarily be realized within the built-up village boundaries. This keeps the village itself manageable and preserves nature and the landscape.

127316.explanation: This is a good goal, but people must be able to perform social work. There must be enough time left to follow training courses and apply for jobs. Also, there must be no negative impact on their disposable social assistance benefits.

127317.explanation: Hart voor Wijchen believes that residents with a low income should be helped. However, the municipality should not stop the exemption from taxes.

127320.explanation: This is unequal treatment compared to people who cannot drive electrically. We support building a park-and-ride facility where this can be made possible.

127321.explanation: This is pampering of the highest order again. If speed must be limited, it should not be on the thoroughfares but rather in residential areas themselves, in the center and home zones.

127322.explanation: We do not always realize this luxury due to the limited number of people working in healthcare. If someone does not want to, they will have to arrange the care themselves. People in healthcare must speak good Dutch.

127325.explanation: The costs must be covered and the facilities maintained. The costs for collecting the fees are limited. The current scheme works if people also pay the dog tax and register their new dogs.

127326.explanation: Tourists are important for the municipality and especially for entrepreneurs. Tourists bring money in, but also liveliness to Wijchen and our centers.

127329.explanation: The urgency for residents and refugees must be treated equally. Both have a roof over their heads that is not their own and therefore not pleasant, but the urgency can be equally high or sometimes higher for residents. This can only be solved with sufficient housing construction and limiting inflow.

127331.explanation: This cannot and should not be prohibited, because they are allowed to farm and energy farmers are also a green alternative as farming entrepreneurs.

127335.explanation: For us, our own swimming pool is important.

127285.explanation: Emilia stands for quality governance over symbolic balance. We appoint people based on skill and expertise, not on gender. A vote for Emilia means a government that makes decisions decisively, focusing on results without compromising on the quality of leadership.

127286.explanation: Emilia focuses on structural participation. We want a municipality where residents truly have influence on decisions that directly affect them. Referenda are an essential part of direct democracy. This strengthens residents' involvement.

127289.explanation: Emilia supports farmers as entrepreneurs but believes that side activities should be assessed per location. Spatial planning, living environment, and nature must remain balanced. Not everything can be everywhere, but customization is possible.

127290.explanation: Emilia focuses on local ownership and participation in the energy transition. Within 12 months, we want to implement two workable models for local ownership and revenue sharing. You will find detailed plans for this in our program.

127291.explanation: Emilia understands the economic benefits of logistics and distribution for the region but advocates a regional approach in developing distribution centers. It is very important to cooperate more with surrounding municipalities. This ensures we make well-considered decisions.

127292.explanation: Emilia has a realistic approach to reception and integration. The focus should not be on reception itself but rather on issues regarding integration, capacity, and safety. Emilia aims for a small-scale, thoughtful, and dignified reception strategy.

127294.explanation: Emilia supports farmers as entrepreneurs but believes that side activities should be assessed per location. Spatial planning, living environment, and nature must remain balanced. Not everything can be everywhere, but customization is possible.

127297.explanation: Emilia is against organizing large fireworks shows on a municipal scale due to the strain on enforcement and environmental impact. However, we do support safe and responsible initiatives such as fireworks moments for associations that can take place with prior approval.

127298.explanation: Emilia acknowledges that free bulky waste collection is unrealistic in the long term. Therefore, we aim for more collection moments for bulky waste and want to pilot small garbage trucks to work more efficiently in narrow streets. This provides better service for residents.

127299.explanation: Emilia believes noise nuisance must be reduced, especially in densely populated areas. We strive for a balance between events and livability.

127300.explanation: Emilia supports inclusivity and equal opportunities. We want to help entrepreneurs make their buildings accessible so that everyone can participate in social and economic life.

127301.explanation: We want to investigate the possibilities for farmers but also carefully weigh the impact on the landscape and living environment.

127302.explanation: We invest in equal opportunities. Sports must be accessible to everyone, and we support the participation of vulnerable groups in social activities.

127307.explanation: Emilia believes monuments must be preserved, not only for their historical value but also to strengthen municipal identity. Cutting back on monument care means losing our cultural identity, which harms tourism and municipal pride.

127309.explanation: Emilia is not in favor of more camera surveillance everywhere. Camera use can contribute to safety in specific locations but only if it is targeted, temporary, and in consultation with residents, respecting privacy.

127312.explanation: Emilia finds it logical that residents of Wijchen get priority in allocating social rental housing. This prevents outsiders from increasing pressure on the housing market. However, the system must be transparent and objective so that everyone knows on which criteria assessments are made.

127313.explanation: Emilia supports more senior housing, but these must be more sustainable and future-proof, such as life-course-proof homes with future installations (like heat pumps and solar panels). The municipality must speed up projects and focus on modular building.

127314.explanation: Emilia wants vacation homes primarily to remain for recreation, but in special cases, there can be room for a flexible approach to converting vacation homes to residential use. The preservation of nature in the surroundings must then be clearly assessed.

127315.explanation: Emilia wants to protect the rural areas and focuses on sustainable, inner-city housing construction and beautification of our centers. All this contributes to preserving green and nature, which is crucial for the livability of Wijchen.

127316.explanation: Emilia believes in the value of social involvement, but the work must be appropriate and respectful. Assistance in finding work must be accompanied by good guidance and training opportunities.

127317.explanation: Emilia wants to help low incomes, but instead of stopping exemptions, she wants to better monitor and target the aid. It is important that residents in poverty do not get into further trouble.

127320.explanation: Emilia supports the expansion of public charging points. This is necessary to make electric driving accessible to everyone, including people without a private driveway, and contributes to sustainable mobility.

127321.explanation: Emilia supports 30 km/h within built-up areas. This increases traffic safety, reduces nuisance, and makes neighborhoods and villages safer and more pleasant for children, cyclists, and the elderly.

127322.explanation: Freedom of choice in care is important for inclusion and equality, but the care sector is under great pressure. Emilia advocates a balance between freedom of choice and practical feasibility, given current care capacity.

127325.explanation: Emilia is open to abolishing the dog tax but only if it is financially responsible and there is support among residents. First, it must be clear how the loss of revenue will be compensated.

127326.explanation: Use revenues explicitly for tourism, recreation, nature and green maintenance, and the local economy. Increase transparency by clearly showing where the revenues go, such as green management, culture promotion, and infrastructure.

127329.explanation: Emilia finds housing important for integration but wants this to happen in balance. Young people and other vulnerable groups must also have fair chances at social rental housing.

127331.explanation: Emilia is against a general ban. Solar panels on agricultural land can be possible if carefully integrated and without disproportionately affecting essential food production or the landscape.

127335.explanation: Wijchen must have its own swimming pool. Emilia advocates for a local swimming pool in Wijchen, focusing on traffic safety and accessibility for all residents. Instead of a joint construction with Beuningen, there is sufficient demand and support in Wijchen for its own well-managed swimming pool.

statements.title: Wijchen should not build a new swimming pool by itself, but should build one together with Beuningen.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The residents of Wijchen should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Wijchen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
The care of historic or special old buildings.

statements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Wijchen.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: More small rental and owner-occupied homes for the elderly should be built in Wijchen.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent for people with a low income. In 2026 that is at most €932.93 per month.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
